# UC-22.36.2 · Right-to-erasure propagation completeness across downstream systems

## Description

Enforces that every registered downstream system reports a deletion acknowledgement for each accepted erasure request within seven days. A subject_token that persists past the SLA in any system is a direct Art.17 / CPRA §1798.105 finding.

## Value

Moves the erasure claim from 'we believe it propagated' to 'we proved it propagated, system by system' — exactly the evidence supervisory authorities ask for on first inspection.

## Implementation

(1) Publish a ropa_system_map.csv lookup keyed by ticket_id → list of systems-in-scope; (2) ingest deletion-ack events from each system (DB TTL jobs, DWH deletion manifests, app-level hooks); (3) run hourly; (4) integrate with SOAR to auto-reopen tickets with missing acks. Indexes required: `index=dsar`.

## Detailed Implementation

Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk Enterprise / Splunk Cloud Platform.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: See subcategory preamble..
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Publish a ropa_system_map.csv lookup keyed by ticket_id → list of systems-in-scope; (2) ingest deletion-ack events from each system (DB TTL jobs, DWH deletion manifests, app-level hooks); (3) run hourly; (4) integrate with SOAR to auto-reopen tickets with missing acks.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=dsar subject_token=* action=erasure earliest=-180d
| stats values(system) as acked_systems by subject_token ticket_id
| lookup ropa_system_map.csv ticket_id OUTPUT systems_in_scope
| eval missing=mvfilter(NOT match(acked_systems, system))
| where mvcount(missing)>0
| table ticket_id subject_token systems_in_scope acked_systems missing
```

Understanding this SPL

**Right-to-erasure propagation completeness across downstream systems** — Moves the erasure claim from 'we believe it propagated' to 'we proved it propagated, system by system' — exactly the evidence supervisory authorities ask for on first inspection.

Documented **Data sources**: See subcategory preamble. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Enterprise / Splunk Cloud Platform. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: dsar.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=dsar, time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• `stats` aggregates events by **subject_token ticket_id**, producing one row per unique combination for trend analysis and threshold evaluation.
• Enriches events using `lookup` (lookup definition + optional OUTPUT fields).
• `eval` defines or adjusts **missing** — applying business logic to classify, threshold, or derive compliance-relevant indicators.
• Filters the current rows with `where mvcount(missing)>0` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.

Output fields: acked_systems, missing, subject_token, systems_in_scope, ticket_id.

Step 3 — Validate
Compare with the GRC system and the DSAR or privacy case system of record so response status and exports match the official register.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Matrix of ticket_id × system with green/red cells; aging histogram; single-value for 'in-scope systems with ack in SLA'.

## SPL

```spl
index=dsar subject_token=* action=erasure earliest=-180d
| stats values(system) as acked_systems by subject_token ticket_id
| lookup ropa_system_map.csv ticket_id OUTPUT systems_in_scope
| eval missing=mvfilter(NOT match(acked_systems, system))
| where mvcount(missing)>0
| table ticket_id subject_token systems_in_scope acked_systems missing
```

## Visualization

Matrix of ticket_id × system with green/red cells; aging histogram; single-value for 'in-scope systems with ack in SLA'.
